judgments.comparative_verdict: Comparable — both reports hit all four expectations; Report 1 is marginally firmer in prescribing fail-closed for the rate limiter, while Report 2 hedges ("acceptable"/"may be safer") but adds useful extra defects (missing TTL, unwrapped secondary call).

report_1.evidence:
The catch is a **bare `except Exception`**, so it treats every exception class from `providers.py` identically — it fails over all of them to the secondary vendor... Concrete fix: replace the bare catch with an explicit non-retryable denylist and only fail over the rest. E.g.:
NON_RETRYABLE = (BadRequestError, ContentPolicyError, AuthError)

report_2.evidence: Because it catches the base `Exception`, *every* error class the primary can raise is treated identically — retry on the secondary vendor... Fix: catch only the retryable set, e.g. `except (RateLimitError, RequestTimeoutError, ServerError) as e:`, let `BadRequestError`/`ContentPolicyError`/`AuthError` propagate

report_1.evidence: **Path A** — returns `True` whenever `STAGE` is anything other than the literal `"production"`... **Assessment: fails open by default and gates on an environment-name string.**... Fix: fail closed by default... **Path C** — on `RedisError`, returns `True`. **Assessment: fails open.**... Fix: return `False` on `RedisError` (block...), not `True`.

report_2.evidence: Returns unconditionally whenever `STAGE` is anything other than the exact string `"production"`, including when `STAGE` is **unset**... fail-open by default... **Path C**... `return True`. Assessment: fail-open on Redis outage... for a PII vendor call fail-closed or a local fallback limiter may be safer.

report_1.evidence: **`input_text` is logged verbatim.** This is the full applicant intake document — the sensitive personal data itself... Fix: drop `input_text`... **`metadata` is logged wholesale**... Fix: apply an explicit **allowlist** of metadata keys known safe to log and drop everything else.

report_2.evidence: `input_text` is the **full applicant intake document** — precisely the sensitive personal data — written verbatim to the application logs... Fix: never log `input_text` (log a hash/length/token-count or omit it)... and scrub/whitelist `metadata` since it may also carry PII.

report_1.evidence: `os.environ.get("PII_SALT", "")` **silently falls back to an empty salt** when the variable is unset. With an empty salt the function computes an unsalted SHA-256 of the applicant id... a missing required secret (a salt) must **fail closed**: raise if `PII_SALT` is absent

report_2.evidence: if `PII_SALT` is unset (or empty), `salt` defaults to `""`, so the output is an **unsalted SHA-256 of the applicant id** — and it does so **silently**, with no error or warning... Fix: fail loudly if `PII_SALT` is missing/empty (raise at startup)
